title: Polymarket - Open App
meta_description: polymarket - open the official prediction-market app to browse live odds, trade outcome shares, and verify market rules before acting.
hero_h2: Trade live event odds with transparent market rules
hero_lead: Polymarket opens the official prediction-market app for live event odds, outcome shares, market rules, and settlement details.
what_is: Polymarket is a prediction-market app for trading event outcomes, with an international CLOB that matches orders offchain and settles matched trades on Polygon. Its official trading docs describe EIP-712 signed orders, non-custodial trading, and onchain settlement.

how.d: Use the verified official domain, then browse topics such as crypto, sports, politics, tech, and culture.
Open a market, review its title, resolution source, end date, and edge cases before placing any order.
Use the official deposit flow; the international docs describe pUSD as an ERC-20 collateral token on Polygon backed by USDC.
Follow price changes, manage positions, and redeem eligible winning tokens after the market resolves.

points.p: Browse live probabilities across public events and trade outcome shares only after checking each market's resolution criteria.
Orders are matched offchain and matched trades settle on Polygon, according to the trading docs.
Polymarket operates through separate legal entities, including Polymarket US at polymarket.us; check availability and local rules before trading.

faq.a: Polymarket is used to browse and trade event outcome markets, where prices reflect market-implied probabilities and each market has its own resolution rules.

faq.q: Is this polymarket page the official app?
faq.a: No. This is an app-forward landing page; use polymarket.com and verify the domain before connecting, funding, or trading.
For the international platform, Polymarket publishes contracts on Polygon mainnet and describes pUSD as an ERC-20 collateral token backed by USDC.
When an event outcome is known, markets resolve according to their rules; Polymarket docs describe UMA Optimistic Oracle resolution and redemption for winning positions.
Polymarket states that Polymarket US is operated by QCX LLC d/b/a Polymarket US, a CFTC-regulated Designated Contract Market; check eligibility for your jurisdiction.

footer_note: Not the official app; verify polymarket.com or polymarket.us where applicable; educational only, not financial advice.
